text:
MEDICAL ONCOLOGY FOLLOW-UP NOTE
Department of Medical Oncology

Patient: Diane Castellano
DOB: [on file]
MRN: [on file]
Attending: [on file]
Visit type: Established patient, oncology follow-up

---

CHIEF COMPLAINT
Established melanoma patient, here for 4-week on-treatment assessment following initiation of Tafinlar + Mekinist. Here to evaluate treatment response, tolerability, and labs.

---

HISTORY OF PRESENT ILLNESS
Ms. Castellano is a 55-year-old female landscape architect residing in the southwestern US who presented several weeks ago with a newly diagnosed stage IV (M1c) nodular melanoma of the back. Breslow depth was 6.1 mm with ulceration and LVI. She underwent WLE and SLNB, which showed 3/4 sentinel nodes positive with extracapsular extension. Staging CT of chest/abdomen/pelvis demonstrated bilateral inguinal and axillary lymphadenopathy, pulmonary micronodules, eight hepatic lesions (largest approximately 3.7 cm), and subcutaneous nodules. Brain MRI was negative for intracranial disease. LDH at diagnosis was elevated. NGS confirmed BRAF V600E at VAF of 48%, along with CDKN2A/2B deletion, TP53, PTEN loss, RAC1, MAP2K1 amplification, TERT promoter, and RB1 LOH. TMB was 18.4 mut/Mb; MSS. PD-L1 8% by IHC.

Given BRAF V600E-mutated stage IV disease with high hepatic burden and elevated LDH, she was started on dabrafenib (Tafinlar) 150 mg BID + trametinib (Mekinist) 2 mg daily approximately 4 weeks ago as first-line targeted therapy.

Today she returns for her 4-week on-therapy check. She reports a striking subjective improvement. Her palpable subcutaneous nodules, which were previously prominent on the trunk and visible as raised nodules, have "almost completely flattened" in her words. She has noticed the lumps are barely palpable now. She feels considerably better than she did at the time of diagnosis.

Side effects: She endorses mild fatigue, which she rates as 2/10 in severity and describes as manageable -- she has been able to continue most of her usual activities. She reports one episode of fever approximately 10 days into therapy, with a peak temperature of 38.1°C, which was self-limited and resolved without intervention over 24 hours. She was told to hold dabrafenib if fever recurred above 38.5°C, and no further febrile episodes have occurred. She denies rash, photosensitivity, edema, nausea, diarrhea, myalgias, or visual changes. She has not needed to hold or reduce doses.

No new symptoms suggestive of CNS involvement (no headache, no focal deficits, no visual disturbance).

---

REVIEW OF SYSTEMS
(+) Fatigue, mild
(+) Single self-limited low-grade fever ~10 days ago, resolved
(-) Headache, visual changes, focal neurological symptoms
(-) Rash, skin thickening, or new skin lesions noted by patient
(-) Nausea, vomiting, diarrhea, constipation
(-) Cough, dyspnea, hemoptysis
(-) Abdominal pain, jaundice
(-) Edema lower extremities
(-) Arthralgias, myalgias
(-) Weight loss (patient reports weight is stable)

---

PAST MEDICAL HISTORY
- Stage IV nodular melanoma of back (BRAF V600E+), diagnosed recently -- see HPI
- No prior history of melanoma or other skin malignancies
- No significant prior medical history; no hypertension, diabetes, cardiac disease, or pulmonary disease documented
- No prior immunotherapy or targeted therapy

PAST SURGICAL HISTORY
- Wide local excision and sentinel lymph node biopsy (recent, as above)

FAMILY HISTORY
- No family history of melanoma
- No family history of breast, ovarian, colorectal, or other known hereditary cancers per patient report

SOCIAL HISTORY
- Works as landscape architect; significant outdoor occupational sun exposure historically
- Lives in the southwestern US; reports intermittent recreational sun exposure
- Non-smoker
- Rare alcohol use
- No illicit drug use
- Lives with partner; supportive social situation

ALLERGIES
- NKDA (no known drug allergies)

MEDICATIONS
- Dabrafenib (Tafinlar) 150 mg PO BID
- Trametinib (Mekinist) 2 mg PO daily
- Ondansetron 4 mg PO PRN nausea (prescribed at initiation, reports minimal use)
- Vitamin D supplementation (self-initiated)

---

PHYSICAL EXAMINATION
Vitals: BP 118/74, HR 72, Temp 36.9°C, SpO2 98% on room air, Wt [on file]
ECOG performance status: 0 (improved from 1 at diagnosis)

General: Well-appearing, well-nourished female in no acute distress. Appears better than at first visit. Affect bright.

Skin: Multiple prior surgical sites on back healing well. Subcutaneous nodules on the trunk, previously firm and raised, are now markedly reduced -- barely palpable. One small area on the left flank that was previously approximately 1.5 cm is now < 5 mm and soft. No new skin lesions, no keratoacanthomas, no palmar-plantar erythrodysesthesia. No significant photosensitivity reaction.

HEENT: No icterus. No oral lesions. Pupils equal and reactive.

Neck: No cervical or supraclavicular lymphadenopathy appreciated.

Lymph nodes: Bilateral inguinal lymphadenopathy, previously prominent, now noticeably reduced bilaterally. Previously the right inguinal node was estimated at roughly 2 cm; today it is approximately 0.8 cm and soft. Axillary nodes not prominently enlarged on exam today.

Chest/CV: Clear to auscultation bilaterally. Regular rate and rhythm, no murmurs.

Abdomen: Soft, non-tender. No hepatomegaly appreciated on exam today (previously liver edge was mildly palpable). No splenomegaly.

Neurological: Alert and oriented x3. No focal deficits. CN II-XII grossly intact. Gait normal.

Extremities: No edema. No calf tenderness.

---

LABORATORY RESULTS
Repeat labs drawn today:
- LDH: 198 U/L (normalized from elevated baseline at diagnosis)
- CBC: WBC 6.2, Hgb 12.8, Plt 221 (all within normal limits)
- CMP: Na 138, K 4.1, Cr 0.9, BUN 14, ALT 32, AST 28, T.bili 0.7, Alk phos 88 -- hepatic function within normal limits
- CK: pending at time of dictation
- Thyroid function: TSH pending

Note: LFTs show marked improvement consistent with hepatic response to therapy. LDH normalization is reassuring.

---

IMAGING
No repeat imaging performed at this visit per plan (first restaging CT planned at 3 months per standard protocol). Most recent imaging was staging CT at diagnosis showing 8 hepatic lesions (largest 3.7 cm), bilateral LN involvement, and subcutaneous disease; brain MRI was negative.

---

ASSESSMENT AND PLAN

1. Stage IV (M1c) BRAF V600E-mutant nodular melanoma -- MARKED CLINICAL RESPONSE to Tafinlar/Mekinist at 4 weeks.
Patient is demonstrating an impressive and rapid clinical response. Subcutaneous nodules nearly resolved on exam. LDH has normalized from elevated baseline. Bilateral inguinal lymphadenopathy substantially reduced. This degree of response at 4 weeks is consistent with what we see with BRAF/MEK-directed therapy in BRAF-mutant disease and is encouraging. No evidence of CNS progression clinically.
- Continue dabrafenib 150 mg BID + trametinib 2 mg daily without dose modification at this time.
- Routine 3-month restaging CT C/A/P scheduled. Will repeat brain MRI at that time given prior hepatic and LN involvement.
- Continue to monitor LFTs, LDH, CBC, CMP at each visit.
- Reinforced skin surveillance -- patient aware to report new skin lesions, warts, or squamous cell papillomas given risk of paradoxical MAPK activation.

2. Pyrexia -- single episode, Grade 1, self-limited.
The isolated low-grade fever (~38.1°C) approximately 10 days ago is a recognized effect of dabrafenib. It was self-limited and did not require dose interruption. Patient has been appropriately counseled: hold dabrafenib if fever ≥ 38.5°C, take acetaminophen, hydrate, and call the office. No further febrile episodes since. No dose change at this time. Monitoring closely -- if febrile episodes recur or escalate, would consider dose reduction per standard guidelines.

3. Fatigue -- mild, Grade 1.
Mild fatigue is a known effect of MEK inhibition and combination targeted therapy. Does not meet threshold for dose modification. Encouraged activity as tolerated. Reviewed energy conservation strategies.

4. ECOG PS now 0.
Significant functional improvement from baseline. Patient able to maintain normal activities.

Patient verbalized understanding of all instructions. Written materials provided. She was instructed to call with any fevers above 38.5°C, new or worsening skin changes, rash, visual changes, or any new concerning symptoms. She will be seen again in approximately 4-8 weeks or sooner PRN.

Time spent: ~35 minutes, more than half spent counseling and coordination.

[Electronically signed -- Attending, Medical Oncology]
